assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 4 ('Understand the Code'), the plan explicitly lists all 4 files for inspection: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (line 62), (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs' (line 68), (3) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs' -- referenced as 'AdvisorySummary struct to understand the severity field' (line 74), and (4) 'common/src/error.rs' -- referenced as 'Read AppError enum to understand error handling pattern' (line 80). All 4 of the specified files are mentioned.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 9 ('Scope containment'), lines 148-155, the plan lists exactly 6 files that would be changed: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs (Files to Modify), and advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs (Files to Create). These match exactly the Files to Modify (3 files, plan.md lines 123-125) and Files to Create (3 files, plan.md lines 129-131) sections. The file descriptions (file-1 through file-6) correspond to these same 6 files. No unrelated files are modified.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 129-131 list: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs -- SeveritySummary response struct' (detailed in file-1-description.md), (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs -- GET handler' (detailed in file-4-description.md), and (3) 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs -- Integration tests' (detailed in file-6-description.md). All three Files to Create are present.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 123-125 list: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs -- Add severity_summary method to AdvisoryService' (detailed in file-3-description.md), (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs -- Register the new route' (detailed in file-5-description.md), and (3) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs -- Add pub mod severity_summary;' (detailed in file-2-description.md). All three Files to Modify are present.

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md lines 185-193 show the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' (Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope 'advisory', and description). The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201' which references the Jira ticket.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md line 195 states: "With flag: `--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'`"

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md line 9 states 'Branch to create: TC-9201'. Step 5 (lines 116-119) shows 'git checkout main' then 'git checkout -b TC-9201'. Step 10 (line 201) shows 'git push -u origin TC-9201' and 'gh pr create --base main'. The target branch 'main' is referenced both in the task summary (line 8: 'Target Branch: main') and in the PR creation command (line 201: '--base main').

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: In conventions.md, the 'Error handling' section (lines 42-44) states: 'All error handling uses Result<T, AppError> pattern' and 'Error wrapping uses .context("descriptive message") from the anyhow style, matching common/src/error.rs'. The 'Module structure' section (lines 22-24) states: 'Each domain module follows the model/ + service/ + endpoints/ structure (observed in sbom/, advisory/, package/)'. Both patterns are explicitly identified from sibling code analysis.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1 (line 26) explicitly states 'Target Branch: main' under parsed sections. Line 8 in the task summary also states 'Target Branch: main'. The branch checkout command in Step 5 (line 117) uses 'git checkout main' and the PR creation in Step 10 (line 201) uses '--base main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1.5 (lines 38-43) is titled 'Description Digest Verification' and states: 'No digest comment provided. Warning:' followed by '"No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced."' and 'Proceeding normally (backward compatibility).' This is also echoed in conventions.md lines 14-18 with the same warning and backward compatibility note.
